translations.text: And they said, Back there! And they said [again], This one came to sojourn, and he must be a judge? Now we will deal worse with thee than with them. And they pressed hard on the man — on Lot; and drew near to break the door.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men stretched out their hand, and brought Lot into the house to them, and shut the door.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they smote the men that were at the entrance of the house with blindness, from the smallest to the greatest; and they wearied themselves to find the entrance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men said to Lot, Whom hast thou here besides? a son-in-law, and thy sons, and thy daughters, and all whom thou hast in the city — bring [them] out of the place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we are going to destroy this place, because the cry of them is great before Jehovah, and Jehovah has sent us to destroy it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Lot went out, and spoke to his sons-in-law, who had married his daughters, and said, Up, go out of this place, for Jehovah will destroy the city. But he was as if he jested, in the sight of his sons-in-law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as the dawn arose, the angels urged Lot, saying, Up, take thy wife and thy two daughters who are present, lest thou perish in the iniquity of the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he lingered, the men laid hold on his hand, and on the hand of his wife, and on the hand of his two daughters, Jehovah being merciful to him; and they led him out, and set him without the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when they had brought them outside, that he said, Escape for thy life: look not behind thee, neither stay thou in all the plain: escape to the mountain, lest thou perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Lot said to them, Not [so], I pray thee, Lord;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold now, thy servant has found favour in thine eyes, and thou hast magnified thy goodness, which thou hast shewn to me in preserving my soul alive; but I cannot escape to the mountain, lest calamity lay hold on me, that I die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold now, this city is near to flee to, and it is small: I pray thee, let me escape thither — is it not small? — and my soul shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Behold, I have accepted thee concerning this thing also, that I will not overthrow the city of which thou hast spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Haste, escape thither; for I cannot do anything until thou art come there. Therefore the name of the city is called Zoar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sun rose upon the earth when Lot came to Zoar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah rained on Sodom and Gomorrah brimstone and fire from Jehovah out of heaven,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and overthrew those cities, and all the plain, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew upon the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his wife looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham rose early in the morning [and went] to the place where he had stood before Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he looked toward Sodom and Gomorrah, and toward all the land of the plain, and beheld, and lo, a smoke went up from the land as the smoke of a furnace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass whenGod destroyed the cities of the plain, thatGod remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities in which Lot dwelt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Lot went up from Zoar, and dwelt in the mountain, and his two daughters with him; for he feared to dwell in Zoar. And he dwelt in a cave, he and his two daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first-born said to the younger, Our father is old, and there is not a man in the land to come in to us after the manner of all the earth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: come, let us give our father wine to drink, and let us lie with him, that we may preserve seed alive of our father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave their father wine to drink that night. And the first-born went in, and lay with her father, and he did not know of her lying down, nor of her rising.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the next day that the first-born said to the younger, Lo, I lay last night with my father: let us give him wine to drink to-night also, and go thou in, lie with him, that we may preserve seed alive of our father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave their father wine to drink that night also. And the younger arose, and lay with him; and he did not know of her lying down, nor of her rising.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And both the daughters of Lot were with child by their father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first-born bore a son, and called his name Moab: the same is the father of the Moabites to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the younger, she also bore a son, and called his name Ben-ammi; the same is the father of the children of Ammon to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham departed thence towards the south country, and dwelt between Kadesh and Shur, and sojourned at Gerar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife, She is my sister. And Abimelech the king of Gerar sent and took Sarah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him, Behold, thou art [but] a dead man, because of the woman that thou hast taken; for she is a man's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Abimelech had not come near her. And he said, Lord, wilt thou also kill a righteous nation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did he not say to me, She is my sister? and she, even she said, He is my brother. In the integrity of my heart and in the innocency of my hands have I done this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said to him in a dream, I also knew that thou didst this in the integrity of thy heart, and I, too, have withheld thee from sinning against me: therefore have I not suffered thee to touch her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, restore the man's wife; for he is a prophet, and will pray for thee, that thou mayest live. And if thou do not restore [her], know that thou shalt certainly die, thou and all that is thine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and spoke all these words in their ears; and the men were greatly afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech called Abraham and said to him, What hast thou done to us? And in what have I sinned against thee, that thou hast brought on me, and on my kingdom, a great sin? Thou hast done to me deeds that ought not to be done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech said to Abraham, What hast thou seen that thou hast done this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham said, Because I said, Surely the fear ofGod is not in this place, and they will kill me for my wife's sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she is also truly my sister, the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass whenGod caused me to wander from my father's house, that I said to her, Let this be thy kindness which thou shalt shew to me: at every place whither we shall come, say of me, He is my brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and bondmen and bondwomen, and gave [them] to Abraham, and restored him Sarah his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech said, Behold, my land is before thee: dwell where it is good in thine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to Sarah he said, Behold, I have given thy brother a thousand [pieces] of silver; behold, let that be to thee a covering of the eyes, in respect of all that are with thee, and with all; and she was reproved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham prayed toGod, andGod healed Abimelech, and his wife and his handmaids, and they bore [children].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah had fast closed up all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah Abraham's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah visited Sarah as he had said, and Jehovah did to Sarah as he had spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sarah conceived, and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the appointed time of whichGod had spoken to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore to him, Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac, being eight days old, asGod had commanded him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sarah said,God has made me laugh: all that hear will laugh with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, Who would have said to Abraham, Sarah will suckle children? For I have borne [him] a son in his old age.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the child grew, and was weaned. And Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, mocking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to Abraham, Cast out this handmaid and her son; for the son of this handmaid shall not inherit with my son — with Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the thing was very grievous in Abraham's sight because of his son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said to Abraham, Let it not be grievous in thy sight because of the lad and because of thy handmaid: [in] all that Sarah hath said to thee hearken to her voice, for in Isaac shall a seed be called to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But also the son of the handmaid will I make a nation, because he is thy seed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham rose up early in the morning, and took bread, and a flask of water, and gave [it] to Hagar, putting [it] on her shoulder — and the child, and sent her away. And she departed, and wandered about in the wilderness of Beer-sheba.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the water was exhausted from the flask; and she cast the child under one of the shrubs,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and she went and sat down over against [him], a bow-shot off; for she said, Let me not behold the death of the child. And she sat over against [him], and lifted up her voice and wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod heard the voice of the lad. And the Angel ofGod called to Hagar from the heavens, and said to her, What [aileth] thee, Hagar? Fear not; forGod hath heard the voice of the lad there, where he is.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, take the lad, and hold him in thy hand; for I will make of him a great nation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water; and she went and filled the flask with water, and gave the lad drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod was with the lad, and he grew; and he dwelt in the wilderness, and became an archer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Paran. And his mother took him a wife out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at that time that Abimelech, and Phichol the captain of his host, spoke to Abraham, saying,God is with thee in all that thou doest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now swear to me here byGod that thou wilt not deal deceitfully with me, nor with my son, nor with my grandson. According to the kindness that I have done to thee, thou shalt do to me, and to the land in which thou sojournest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham said, I will swear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham reproved Abimelech because of a well of water that Abimelech's servants had violently taken away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech said, I do not know who has done this, neither hast thou told me [of it], neither have I heard [of it] but to-day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham took sheep and oxen, and gave them to Abimelech; and both of them made a covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham set seven ewe-lambs of the flock by themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech said to Abraham, What [mean] these seven ewe-lambs, these which thou hast set by themselves?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, That thou take the seven ewe-lambs of my hand, that they may be a witness to me that I have dug this well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore he called that place Beer-sheba, because there they had sworn, both of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made a covenant at Beer-sheba. And Abimelech rose up, and Phichol the captain of his host, and returned into the land of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [Abraham] planted a tamarisk in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of Jehovah, the EternalGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham sojourned in the Philistines' land many days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these things, thatGod tried Abraham, and said to him, Abraham! and he said, Here am I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Take now thy son, thine only [son], whom thou lovest, Isaac, and get thee into the land of Moriah, and there offer him up for a burnt-offering on one of the mountains which I will tell thee of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham rose early in the morning, and saddled his ass, and took two of his young men with him, and Isaac his son; and he clave the wood for the burnt-offering, and rose up and went to the place thatGod had told him of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes and saw the place from afar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham said to his young men, Abide ye here with the ass; and I and the lad will go yonder and worship, and come again to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham took the wood of the burnt-offering, and laid it on Isaac his son; and he took the fire in his hand, and the knife, and they went both of them together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac spoke to Abraham his father, and said, My father! And he said, Here am I, my son. And he said, Behold the fire and the wood; but where is the sheep for a burnt-offering?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham said, My son,God will provide himself with the sheep for a burnt-offering. And they went both of them together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came to the place of whichGod had told him. And Abraham built the altar there, and piled the wood; and he bound Isaac his son, and laid him on the altar upon the wood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham stretched out his hand, and took the knife to slaughter his son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah called to him from the heavens, and said, Abraham, Abraham! And he said, Here am I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Stretch not out thy hand against the lad, neither do anything to him; for now I know that thou fearestGod, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only [son], from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold, behind was a ram caught in the thicket by its horns; and Abraham went and took the ram, and offered it up for a burnt-offering instead of his son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham called the name of that place Jehovah-jireh; as it is said at the present day, On the mount of Jehovah will be provided.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel of Jehovah called to Abraham from the heavens a second time,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, By myself I swear, saith Jehovah, that, because thou hast done this, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only [son],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will richly bless thee, and greatly multiply thy seed, as the stars of heaven, and as the sand that is on the sea-shore; and thy seed shall possess the gate of his enemies;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth bless themselves, because thou hast hearkened to my voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham returned to his young men, and they rose up and went together to Beer-sheba. And Abraham dwelt at Beer-sheba.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying, Behold, Milcah, she also has borne sons to thy brother Nahor:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Uz his first-born, and Buz his brother, and Kemuel the father of Aram,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Chesed, and Hazo, and Pildash, and Jidlaph, and Bethuel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (And Bethuel begot Rebecca.) These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham's brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his concubine, named Reumah, she also bore Tebah, and Gaham, and Thahash, and Maacah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the life of Sarah was a hundred and twenty-seven years: [these were] the years of the life of Sarah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sarah died in Kirjath-Arba: that is Hebron, in the land of Canaan. And Abraham came to mourn for Sarah, and to weep for her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham rose up from before his dead, and spoke to the sons of Heth, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am a stranger and a sojourner with you; give me a possession of a sepulchre with you, that I may bury my dead from before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Heth answered Abraham, saying to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear us, my lord: thou art a prince ofGod among us: in the choicest of our sepulchres bury thy dead: none of us shall withhold from thee his sepulchre for burying thy dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham rose up, and bowed down to the people of the land, to the sons of Heth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and spoke to them, saying, If it be your will that I should bury my dead from before me, hear me, and entreat for me Ephron the son of Zohar,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that he may give me the cave of Machpelah, which is his, which is at the end of his field; for the full money let him give it to me amongst you for a possession of a sepulchre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ephron was dwelling among the sons of Heth. And Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham, in the ears of the sons of Heth, [even] of all that went in at the gate of his city, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No, my lord: hear me. The field give I thee; and the cave that is in it, to thee I give it; before the eyes of the sons of my people give I it thee: bury thy dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham bowed down before the people of the land;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he spoke to Ephron, in the ears of the people of the land, saying, But if only thou wouldst listen to me, I give the money for the field: take [it] of me, and I will bury my dead there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ephron answered Abraham, saying to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My lord, hearken to me. A field of four hundred shekels of silver, what is that between me and thee? bury therefore thy dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham hearkened to Ephron; and Abraham weighed to Ephron the money that he had named in the ears of the sons of Heth — four hundred shekels of silver, current with the merchant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So the field of Ephron, which was at Machpelah, which was before Mamre, the field, and the cave that was in it, and all the trees that were in the field, that were in all its borders round about,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: were assured to Abraham for a possession before the eyes of the sons of Heth, before all who went in at the gate of his city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field at Machpelah, opposite to Mamre: that is Hebron, in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the field and the cave that was in it were assured to Abraham for a possession of a sepulchre by the sons of Heth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham was old, [and] advanced in age; and Jehovah had blessed Abraham in all things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham said to his servant, the eldest of his house, who ruled over all that he had, Put thy hand, I pray thee, under my thigh,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will make thee swear by Jehovah, theGod of the heavens and theGod of the earth, that thou take not a wife for my son of the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I am dwelling;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but thou shalt go to my land and to my kindred, and take a wife for my son Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servant said to him, Perhaps the woman will not be willing to follow me to this land: must I, then, bring thy son again in any case to the land from which thou hast removed?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham said to him, Beware that thou bring not my son thither again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah theGod of the heavens, who took me out of my father's house, and out of the land of my nativity, and who has spoken to me, and who has sworn to me, saying, Unto thy seed will I give this land — he will send his angel before thee, that thou mayest take a wife for my son thence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if the woman be not willing to follow thee, then thou shalt be quit of this my oath: only, bring not my son thither again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master, and swore unto him concerning that matter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servant took ten camels of the camels of his master, and departed; now all the treasure of his master was under his hand; and he arose and went to Aram-naharaim, to the city of Nahor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the camels kneel down outside the city by a well of water, at the time of the evening, when the women came out to draw [water].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Jehovah,God of my master Abraham, meet me, I pray thee, [with thy blessing] this day, and deal kindly with my master Abraham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I stand [here] by the well of water, and the daughters of the men of the city come out to draw water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let it come to pass, [that] the maiden to whom I shall say, Let down thy pitcher, I pray thee, that I may drink, and who will say, Drink, and I will give thy camels drink also, be she whom thou hast appointed for thy servant Isaac; and hereby I shall know that thou hast dealt kindly with my master.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass before he had ended speaking, that behold, Rebecca came out, who was born to Bethuel, son of Milcah the wife of Nahor, Abraham's brother; and [she had] her pitcher upon her shoulder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the maiden was very fair in countenance; a virgin, and no man had known her. And she went down to the well, and filled her pitcher, and came up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servant ran to meet her, and said, Let me, I pray thee, sip a little water out of thy pitcher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, Drink, my lord! And she hasted and let down her pitcher on her hand, and gave him to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when she had given him enough to drink, she said, I will draw [water] for thy camels also, until they have drunk enough.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she hasted and emptied her pitcher into the trough, and ran again to the well to draw [water]; and she drew for all his camels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man was astonished at her, remaining silent, to know whether Jehovah had made his journey prosperous or not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the camels had drunk enough, that the man took a gold ring, of half a shekel weight, and two bracelets for her hands, ten [shekels] weight of gold,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Whose daughter art thou? tell me, I pray thee. Is there room [in] thy father's house for us to lodge?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to him, I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to him, There is straw, and also much provender with us; also room to lodge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man stooped, and bowed down before Jehovah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Blessed be Jehovah,God of my master Abraham, who has not withdrawn his loving-kindness and his faithfulness from my master; I being in the way, Jehovah has led me to the house of my master's brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the maiden ran and told these things to her mother's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rebecca had a brother, named Laban; and Laban ran out to the man, to the well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he saw the ring and the bracelets on his sister's hand, and when he heard the words of Rebecca his sister, saying, Thus spoke the man to me — that he came to the man, and behold, he was standing by the camels, by the well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Come in, blessed of Jehovah! why standest thou outside? for I have prepared the house, and room for the camels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man came into the house; and he ungirded the camels, and gave the camels straw and provender, and water to wash his feet, and the feet of the men who were with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was set [meat] before him to eat; but he said, I will not eat until I have made known my business. And he said, Speak on.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I am Abraham's servant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah has blessed my master greatly, and he is become great; and he has given him sheep and cattle, and silver and gold, and bondmen and bondwomen, and camels and asses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sarah, my master's wife, bore a son to my master after she had grown old; and unto him has he given all that he has.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my master made me swear, saying, Thou shalt not take a wife for my son of the daughters of the Canaanite, in whose land I am dwelling;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but thou shalt by all means go to my father's house and to my family, and take a wife for my son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said to my master, Perhaps the woman will not follow me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to me, Jehovah, before whom I have walked, will send his angel with thee, and prosper thy way, that thou mayest take a wife for my son of my family, and out of my father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shalt thou be quit of my oath, when thou shalt have come to my family. And if they give thee not [one], thou shalt be quit of my oath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I came this day to the well, and said, Jehovah,God of my master Abraham, if now thou wilt prosper my way on which I go,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold, I stand by the well of water, and let it come to pass that the damsel who cometh forth to draw [water], and to whom I shall say, Give me, I pray thee, a little water out of thy pitcher to drink,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and she shall say to me, Both drink thou, and I will also draw for thy camels — that she should be the woman whom Jehovah hath appointed for my master's son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before I ended speaking in my heart, behold, Rebecca came forth with her pitcher on her shoulder, and went down to the well, and drew [water]; and I said to her, Give me, I pray thee, to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she hasted and let down her pitcher from her [shoulder], and said, Drink, and I will give thy camels drink also. And I drank; and she gave the camels drink also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I asked her, and said, Whose daughter art thou? And she said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor's son, whom Milcah bore to him. And I put the ring on her nose, and the bracelets on her hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I stooped, and bowed down before Jehovah; and I blessed Jehovah,God of my master Abraham, who has led me the right way to take my master's brother's daughter for his son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, if ye will deal kindly and truly with my master, tell me; and if not, tell me; and I will turn to the right hand or to the left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban and Bethuel answered and said, The thing proceeds from Jehovah: we cannot speak to thee bad or good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, Rebecca is before thee: take [her], and go away; and let her be wife of thy master's son, as Jehovah has said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Abraham's servant heard their words, that he bowed down to the earth before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servant brought forth silver articles, and gold articles, and clothing, and he gave [them] to Rebecca; and he gave to her brother, and to her mother, precious things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they ate and drank, he and the men that were with him, and lodged. And they rose up in the morning; and he said, Send me away to my master.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her brother and her mother said, Let the maiden abide with us [some] days, or [say] ten; after that she shall go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Do not hinder me, seeing Jehovah has prospered my way: send me away, and I will go to my master.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Let us call the maiden and inquire at her mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they called Rebecca and said to her, Wilt thou go with this man? And she said, I will go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sent away Rebecca their sister, and her nurse, and Abraham's servant, and his men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they blessed Rebecca, and said to her, Thou art our sister; mayest thou become thousands of tens of thousands; and may thy seed possess the gate of their enemies!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rebecca arose, and her maids, and they rode upon the camels, and followed the man. And the servant took Rebecca, and went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac had just returned from Beer-lahai-roi; for he was dwelling in the south country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac had gone out to meditate in the fields toward the beginning of evening. And he lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold, camels were coming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rebecca lifted up her eyes and saw Isaac, and she sprang off the camel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she had said to the servant, Who is the man that is walking in the fields to meet us? And the servant said, That is my master! Then she took the veil, and covered herself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servant told Isaac all things that he had done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac led her into his mother Sarah's tent; and he took Rebecca, and she became his wife, and he loved her. And Isaac was comforted after [the death of] his mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham took another wife named Keturah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she bore him Zimran, and Jokshan, and Medan, and Midian, and Ishbak, and Shuah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jokshan begot Sheba and Dedan; and the sons of Dedan were the Asshurim, and the Letushim, and the Leummim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Midian were Ephah, and Epher, and Enoch, and Abida, and Eldaah. All these were sons of Keturah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham gave all that he had to Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to the sons of the concubines that Abraham had, Abraham gave gifts, and, while he yet lived, sent them away from Isaac his son, eastward to the east country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the days of the years of Abraham's life which he lived: a hundred and seventy-five years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abraham expired and died in a good old age, old and full [of days]; and was gathered to his peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar the Hittite, which was opposite to Mamre —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the field that Abraham had purchased of the sons of Heth: there was Abraham buried, and Sarah his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after the death of Abraham, thatGod blessed his son Isaac. And Isaac dwelt at Beer-lahai-roi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the generations of Ishmael, Abraham's son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah's bondwoman, bore to Abraham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the sons of Ishmael by their names according to their generations: Nebaioth, the firstborn of Ishmael; and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hadad and Tema, Jetur, Naphish and Kedmah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names, in their hamlets and their encampments — twelve princes of their peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the years of the life of Ishmael: a hundred and thirty-seven years; and he expired and died, and was gathered to his peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they dwelt from Havilah to Shur, which is opposite to Egypt, as one goes towards Assyria. He settled before the face of all his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the generations of Isaac, Abraham's son. Abraham begot Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebecca as wife, the daughter of Bethuel the Syrian of Padan-Aram, the sister of Laban the Syrian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac entreated Jehovah for his wife, because she was barren; and Jehovah was entreated of him, and Rebecca his wife conceived.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children struggled together within her; and she said, If [it be] so, why am I thus? And she went to inquire of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to her, Two nations are in thy womb, And two peoples shall be separated from thy bowels; And one people shall be stronger than the other people, And the elder shall serve the younger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when her days to be delivered were fulfilled, behold, there were twins in her womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first came out red — all over like a hairy garment; and they called his name Esau.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after that came his brother out; and his hand took hold of Esau's heel; and his name was called Jacob. And Isaac was sixty years old when they were born.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the boys grew, and Esau became a man skilled in hunting, a man of the field; and Jacob was a homely man, dwelling in tents.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac loved Esau, because venison was to his taste; and Rebecca loved Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob had cooked a dish; and Esau came from the field, and he was faint.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau said to Jacob, Feed me, I pray thee, with the red — the red thing there, for I am faint. Therefore was his name called Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said, Sell me now thy birthright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau said, Behold, I am going to die, and of what use can the birthright be to me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said, Swear unto me now. And he swore unto him, and sold his birthright to Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob gave Esau bread and the dish of lentils; and he ate and drank, and rose up and went away. Thus Esau despised the birthright.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a famine in the land, besides the former famine which had been in the days of Abraham. And Isaac went to Abimelech the king of the Philistines, to Gerar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah appeared to him and said, Go not down to Egypt: dwell in the land that I shall tell thee of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sojourn in this land; and I will be with thee and bless thee; for unto thee, and unto thy seed, I will give all these countries; and I will perform the oath which I swore unto Abraham thy father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will multiply thy seed as the stars of heaven, and unto thy seed will I give all these countries; and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth bless themselves —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because that Abraham hearkened to my voice, and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac dwelt at Gerar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of the place asked about his wife. And he said, She is my sister; for he feared to say, my wife, [saying to himself,] Lest the men of the place slay me on account of Rebecca — because she was fair in countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he had been there some time, that Abimelech the king of the Philistines looked out of the window, and saw, and behold, Isaac was dallying with Rebecca his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Abimelech called Isaac, and said, Behold, she is certainly thy wife; and how saidst thou, She is my sister? and Isaac said to him, Because I said, Lest I die on account of her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech said, What is this thou hast done to us? But a little and one of the people might have lain with thy wife, and thou wouldest have brought a trespass on us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech charged all the people, saying, He that touches this man or his wife shall certainly be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac sowed in that land, and received in the same year a hundredfold; and Jehovah blessed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man became great, and he became continually greater, until he was very great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had possessions of flocks, and possessions of herds, and a great number of servants; and the Philistines envied him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the wells that his father's servants had dug in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines stopped them and filled them with earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech said to Isaac, Go from us; for thou art become much mightier than we.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac departed thence, and pitched his camp in the valley of Gerar, and dwelt there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac dug again the wells of water that they had dug in the days of Abraham his father, and that the Philistines had stopped after the death of Abraham; and he called their names after the names by which his father had called them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac's servants dug in the valley, and found there a well of springing water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the shepherds of Gerar strove with Isaac's shepherds, saying, The water is ours. And he called the name of the well Esek, because they had quarrelled with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they dug another well, and they strove for that also; and he called the name of it Sitnah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he removed thence and dug another well; and they did not strive for that. And he called the name of it Rehoboth, and said, For now Jehovah has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went up thence to Beer-sheba.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah appeared to him the same night, and said, I am theGod of Abraham thy father: fear not, for I am with thee, and will bless thee, and multiply thy seed for my servant Abraham's sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built an altar there, and called upon the name of Jehovah. And he pitched his tent there; and there Isaac's servants dug a well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abimelech, and Ahuzzath his friend, and Phichol the captain of his host, went to him from Gerar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac said to them, Why are ye come to me, seeing ye hate me, and have driven me away from you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, We saw certainly that Jehovah is with thee; and we said, Let there be then an oath between us — between us and thee, and let us make a covenant with thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thou wilt do us no wrong, as we have not touched thee, and as we have done to thee nothing but good, and have let thee go in peace; thou art now blessed of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they rose early in the morning, and swore one to another; and Isaac sent them away, and they departed from him in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the same day, that Isaac's servants came, and told him concerning the well that they had dug, and said to him, We have found water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called it Shebah; therefore the name of the city is Beer-sheba to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau was forty years old, when he took as wives Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basmath the daughter of Elon the Hittite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were a grief of mind to Isaac and to Rebecca.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Isaac had become old, and his eyes were dim so that he could not see, that he called Esau his elder son, and said to him, My son! And he said to him, Here am I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Behold now, I am become old; I know not the day of my death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, I pray thee, take thy weapons, thy quiver and thy bow, and go out to the field and hunt me venison,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and prepare me a savoury dish such as I love, and bring it to me that I may eat, in order that my soul may bless thee before I die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rebecca heard when Isaac spoke to Esau his son. And Esau went to the field to hunt venison, to bring it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rebecca spoke to Jacob her son, saying, Behold, I heard thy father speak to Esau thy brother, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bring me venison, and prepare me a savoury dish, that I may eat, and bless thee before Jehovah, before my death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, my son, hearken to my voice in that which I command thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go, I pray thee, to the flock, and fetch me thence two good kids of the goats. And I will make of them a savoury dish for thy father, such as he loves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt bring [it] to thy father, that he may eat, in order that he may bless thee before his death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to Rebecca his mother, Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My father perhaps will feel me, and I shall be in his sight as one who mocks [him], and I shall bring a curse on me, and not a blessing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his mother said to him, On me [be] thy curse, my son! Only hearken to my voice, and go, fetch [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went, and fetched and brought [them] to his mother. And his mother prepared a savoury dish such as his father loved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rebecca took the clothes of her elder son Esau, the costly ones which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob her younger son;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and she put the skins of the kids of the goats on his hands, and on the smooth of his neck;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and she gave the savoury dishes and the bread that she had prepared into the hand of her son Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to his father, and said, My father! And he said, Here am I: who art thou, my son?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to his father, I am Esau, thy firstborn. I have done according as thou didst say to me. Arise, I pray thee, sit and eat of my venison, in order that thy soul may bless me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac said to his son, How is it that thou hast found [it] so quickly, my son? And he said, Because Jehovah thyGod put [it] in my way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac said to Jacob, Come near, I pray thee, that I may feel thee, my son, whether thou be really my son Esau or not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob drew near to Isaac his father; and he felt him, and said, The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did not discern him, because his hands were hairy, as his brother Esau's hands; and he blessed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Art thou really my son Esau? And he said, It is I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Bring [it] near to me, that I may eat of my son's venison, in order that my soul may bless thee. And he brought [it] near to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his father Isaac said to him, Come near, now, and kiss me, my son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came near, and kissed him. And he smelt the smell of his clothes, and blessed him, and said, See, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field which Jehovah hath blessed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod give thee of the dew of heaven, And of the fatness of the earth, And plenty of corn and new wine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let peoples serve thee, And races bow down to thee. Be lord over thy brethren, And let thy mother's sons bow down to thee. Cursed be they that curse thee, And blessed be they that bless thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Isaac had ended blessing Jacob, and when Jacob was only just gone out from Isaac his father, that Esau his brother came from his hunting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he also had prepared savoury dishes, and he brought [them] in to his father, and said to his father, Let my father arise and eat of his son's venison, in order that thy soul may bless me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac his father said to him, Who art thou? And he said, I am thy son, thy firstborn, Esau.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Isaac trembled with exceeding great trembling, and said, Who was he, then, that hunted venison and brought [it] to me? And I have eaten of all before thou camest, and have blessed him; also blessed he shall be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with a great and exceeding bitter cry, and said to his father, Bless me — me also, my father!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Thy brother came with subtilty, and has taken away thy blessing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Is it not therefore he was named Jacob, for he has supplanted me now twice? He took away my birthright, and behold, now he has taken away my blessing. And he said, Hast thou not reserved a blessing for me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac answered and said to Esau, Behold, I have made him lord over thee, and all his brethren have I given to him for servants, and with corn and new wine have I supplied him — and what can I do now for thee, my son?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau said to his father, Hast thou then but one blessing, my father? bless me — me also, my father! And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac his father answered and said to him, Behold, thy dwelling shall be of the fatness of the earth, And of the dew of heaven from above;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by thy sword shalt thou live; And thou shalt serve thy brother; And it shall come to pass when thou rovest about, That thou shalt break his yoke from off thy neck.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him. And Esau said in his heart, The days of mourning for my father are at hand, and I will slay my brother Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the words of Esau her elder son were told to Rebecca. And she sent and called Jacob her younger son, and said to him, Behold, thy brother Esau, as touching thee, comforts himself that he will kill thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, my son, hearken to my voice, and arise, flee to Laban my brother, to Haran;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and abide with him some days, until thy brother's fury turn away —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: until thy brother's anger turn away from thee, and he forget what thou hast done to him; then I will send and fetch thee thence. Why should I be bereaved even of you both in one day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rebecca said to Isaac, I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth. If Jacob take a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these, of the daughters of the land, what good should my life do me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac called Jacob, and blessed him, and charged him, and said to him, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, go to Padan-Aram, to the house of Bethuel thy mother's father, and take a wife thence of the daughters of Laban thy mother's brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the AlmightyGod bless thee, and make thee fruitful and multiply thee, that thou mayest become a company of peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And may he give thee the blessing of Abraham, to thee and to thy seed with thee, in order that thou mayest possess the land of thy sojourning, whichGod gave to Abraham!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac sent away Jacob; and he went to Padan-Aram, to Laban the son of Bethuel the Syrian, the brother of Rebecca, Jacob's and Esau's mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob, and sent him away to Padan-Aram, to take a wife thence, blessing him, and giving him a charge saying, Thou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of Canaan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and [that] Jacob had obeyed his father and his mother, and was gone to Padan-Aram.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau saw that the daughters of Canaan were evil in the sight of Isaac his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau went to Ishmael, and took, besides the wives that he had, Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael Abraham's son, the sister of Nebaioth, to be his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob went out from Beer-sheba, and went towards Haran.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he lighted on a certain place, and lodged there, because the sun had set. And he took [one] of the stones of the place, and made [it] his pillow, and lay down in that place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he dreamed, and behold, a ladder was set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to the heavens. And behold, angels ofGod ascended and descended upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, Jehovah stood above it. And he said, I am Jehovah, theGod of Abraham, thy father, and theGod of Isaac: the land on which thou liest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thy seed shall be as the dust of the earth, and thou shalt spread abroad to the west, and to the east, and to the north, and to the south; and in thee and in thy seed shall all the families of the earth be blessed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, I am with thee, and will keep thee in all [places] to which thou goest, and will bring thee again into this land; for I will not leave thee until I have done what I have spoken to thee of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob awoke from his sleep, and said, Surely Jehovah is in this place, and I knew [it] not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was afraid, and said, How dreadful is this place! this is none other but the house ofGod, and this is the gate of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob rose early in the morning, and took the stone that he had made his pillow, and set it up [for] a pillar, and poured oil on the top of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called the name of that place Beth-el; but the name of that city was Luz at the first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob vowed a vow, saying, IfGod will be with me, and keep me on this road that I go, and will give me bread to eat, and a garment to put on,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I come again to my father's house in peace — then shall Jehovah be myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this stone, which I have set up [for] a pillar, shall beGod's house; and of all that thou wilt give me I will without fail give the tenth to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob continued his journey, and went into the land of the children of the east.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he looked, and behold, there was a well in the fields, and behold there, three flocks of sheep were lying by it; for out of that well they watered the flocks, and a great stone was at the mouth of the well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when all the flocks were gathered there, they rolled the stone from the mouth of the well, and watered the sheep, and put the stone again on the mouth of the well in its place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to them, My brethren, whence are ye? And they said, Of Haran are we.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Do ye know Laban the son of Nahor? And they said, We do know [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Is he well? And they said, [He is] well; and behold, there comes Rachel his daughter with the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Behold, it is yet high day; it is not time that the cattle should be gathered together; water the sheep, and go, feed [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together, and they roll the stone from the mouth of the well, and we water the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he was still speaking to them, Rachel came with her father's sheep, for she was a shepherdess.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mother's brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother's brother, that Jacob went near, and rolled the stone from the mouth of the well, and watered the sheep of Laban his mother's brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob kissed Rachel, and lifted up his voice and wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob told Rachel that he was a brother of her father, and that he was Rebecca's son; and she ran and told her father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Laban heard the tidings of Jacob his sister's son, that he ran to meet him, and embraced him, and kissed him, and brought him to his house; and he told Laban all these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said to him, Thou art indeed my bone and my flesh. And he abode with him a month's time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said to Jacob, Because thou art my brother, shouldest thou serve me for nothing? tell me, what shall be thy wages?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah, and the name of the younger, Rachel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the eyes of Leah were tender; but Rachel was of beautiful form and beautiful countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob loved Rachel, and said, I will serve thee seven years for Rachel thy younger daughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said, It is better that I give her to thee than that I should give her to another man: abide with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob served seven years for Rachel; and they were in his eyes as single days, because he loved her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to Laban, Give [me] my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban gathered together all the men of the place, and made a feast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter, and brought her to him; and he went in to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban gave to her Zilpah, his maidservant, to be maidservant to Leah his daughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the morning, that behold, it was Leah. And he said to Laban, What is this thou hast done to me? Have I not served thee for Rachel? Why then hast thou deceived me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said, It is not so done in our place, to give the younger before the firstborn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fulfil the week [with] this one: then we will give thee the other one also, for the service that thou shalt serve me yet seven other years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob did so, and fulfilled the week [with] this one, and he gave him Rachel his daughter to be his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban gave to Rachel his daughter, Bilhah, his maidservant, to be her maidservant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went in also to Rachel; and he loved also Rachel more than Leah. And he served with him yet seven other years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jehovah saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb; but Rachel was barren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Leah conceived, and bore a son, and called his name Reuben; for she said, Because Jehovah has looked upon my affliction; for now my husband will love me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she again conceived, and bore a son, and said, Because Jehovah has heard that I am hated, he has therefore given me this one also; and she called his name Simeon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she again conceived, and bore a son, and said, Now this time will my husband be united to me, for I have borne him three sons; therefore was his name called Levi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she again conceived, and bore a son, and said, This time will I praise Jehovah; therefore she called his name Judah. And she ceased to bear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister, and said to Jacob, Give me children, or else I die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel, and he said, Am I inGod's stead, who has withheld from thee the fruit of the womb?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, Behold, there is my maid, Bilhah: go in to her, in order that she may bear on my knees, and I may also be built up by her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she gave him Bilhah her maidservant as wife, and Jacob went in to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bilhah conceived, and bore Jacob a son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rachel said,God has done me justice, and has also heard my voice, and given me a son; therefore she called his name Dan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bilhah Rachel's maidservant again conceived, and bore Jacob a second son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rachel said, Wrestlings ofGod have I wrestled with my sister, and have prevailed; and she called his name Naphtali.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Leah saw that she had ceased to bear, she took Zilpah her maidservant and gave her to Jacob as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zilpah Leah's maidservant bore Jacob a son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Leah said, Fortunately! and she called his name Gad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zilpah Leah's maidservant bore Jacob a second son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Leah said, Happy am I; for the daughters will call me blessed! and she called his name Asher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Reuben went out in the days of wheat-harvest, and found mandrakes in the fields; and he brought them to his mother Leah. And Rachel said to Leah, Give me, I pray thee, of thy son's mandrakes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to her, Is it [too] little that thou hast taken my husband, that thou wilt take my son's mandrakes also? And Rachel said, Therefore he shall lie with thee to-night for thy son's mandrakes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jacob came from the fields in the evening, Leah went out to meet him, and said, Thou must come in to me, for indeed I have hired thee with my son's mandrakes. And he lay with her that night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod hearkened to Leah, and she conceived, and bore Jacob a fifth son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Leah said,God has given me my hire, because I have given my maidservant to my husband; and she called his name Issachar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Leah again conceived, and bore Jacob a sixth son;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Leah said,God has endowed me with a good dowry; this time will my husband dwell with me, because I have borne him six sons. And she called his name Zebulun.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards she bore a daughter, and called her name Dinah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod remembered Rachel, andGod listened to her, and opened her womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she conceived, and bore a son, and said,God has taken away my reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she called his name Joseph; and said, Jehovah will add to me another son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Rachel had borne Joseph, that Jacob said to Laban, Send me away, that I may go to my place and to my country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give [me] my wives for whom I have served thee, and my children; that I may go away, for thou knowest my service which I have served thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said to him, I pray thee, if I have found favour in thine eyes — I have discovered that Jehovah has blessed me for thy sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Appoint to me thy wages, and I will give it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Thou knowest how I have served thee, and what thy cattle has become with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it was little that thou hadst before me, and it is increased to a multitude, and Jehovah has blessed thee from the time I came; and now, when shall I also provide for my house?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, What shall I give thee? And Jacob said, Thou shalt not give me anything. If thou doest this for me, I will again feed [and] keep thy flock:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will pass through all thy flock to-day, to remove thence all the speckled and spotted sheep, and all the brown lambs, and the spotted and speckled among the goats; and [that] shall be my hire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my righteousness shall answer for me hereafter, when thou comest about my hire, before thy face: all that is not speckled and spotted among the goats, and brown among the lambs, let that be stolen with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said, Well, let it be according to thy word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he removed that day the he-goats that were ringstraked and spotted, and all the she-goats that were speckled and spotted, every one that had white in it, and all the brown among the lambs, and gave [them] into the hand of his sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put three days' journey between himself and Jacob. And Jacob fed the rest of Laban's flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob took fresh rods of white poplar, almond-tree, and maple; and peeled off white stripes in them, uncovering the white which was on the rods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he set the rods which he had peeled before the flock, in the troughs at the watering-places where the flock came to drink, and they were ardent when they came to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the flock was ardent before the rods; and the flock brought forth ringstraked, speckled, and spotted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob separated the lambs, and set the faces of the flock toward the ringstraked, and all the brown in the flock of Laban; and he made himself separate flocks, and did not put them with Laban's flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass whensoever the strong cattle were ardent, that Jacob laid the rods before the eyes of the flock in the gutters, that they might become ardent among the rods;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but when the sheep were feeble, he put [them] not in; so the feeble were Laban's, and the strong Jacob's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man increased very, very much, and had much cattle, and bondwomen, and bondmen, and camels, and asses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he heard the words of Laban's sons, saying, Jacob has taken away all that was our father's, and of what was our father's he has acquired all this glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob saw the countenance of Laban, and behold, it was not toward him as previously.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Jacob, Return into the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the fields to his flock,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to them, I see your father's countenance, that it is not toward me as previously; but theGod of my father has been with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And you know that with all my power I have served your father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And your father has mocked me, and has changed my wages ten times; butGod suffered him not to hurt me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he said thus; The speckled shall be thy hire, then all the flocks bore speckled; and if he said thus: The ringstraked shall be thy hire, then all the flocks bore ringstraked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod has taken away the cattle of your father, and given [them] to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the time of the ardour of the flocks, that I lifted up mine eyes, and saw in a dream, and behold, the rams that leaped upon the flocks were ringstraked, speckled, and spotted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Angel ofGod said to me in a dream, Jacob! And I said, Here am I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Lift up now thine eyes, and see: all the rams that leap upon the flock are ringstraked, speckled, and spotted; for I have seen all that Laban does to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am theGod of Bethel, where thou anointedst the pillar, where thou vowedst a vow to me. Now arise, depart out of this land, and return to the land of thy kindred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rachel and Leah answered and said to him, Is there yet any portion or inheritance for us in our father's house?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are we not reckoned of him strangers? for he has sold us, and has even constantly devoured our money.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all the wealth thatGod has taken from our father is ours and our children's; and now whateverGod has said to thee do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives upon camels,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and carried away all his cattle, and all his property that he had acquired — the cattle of his possessions that he had acquired in Padan-Aram, to go to Isaac his father, into the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban had gone to shear his sheep. And Rachel stole the teraphim that [belonged] to her father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob deceived Laban the Syrian, in that he did not tell him that he fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he fled with all that he had; and he rose up and passed over the river, and set his face [toward] mount Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob had fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took his brethren with him, and pursued after him seven days' journey, and overtook him on mount Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod came to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night, and said to him, Take care thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban came up with Jacob; and Jacob had pitched his tent on the mountain; Laban also with his brethren pitched on mount Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said to Jacob, What hast thou done, that thou hast deceived me, and hast carried away my daughters as captives of war?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why didst thou flee away covertly, and steal away from me, and didst not tell me, that I might have conducted thee with mirth and with songs, with tambour and with harp;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters? Now thou hast acted foolishly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It would be in the power of my hand to do you hurt; but theGod of your father spoke to me last night, saying, Take care that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now that thou must needs be gone, because thou greatly longedst after thy father's house, why hast thou stolen mygods?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob answered and said to Laban, I was afraid; for I said, Lest thou shouldest take by force thy daughters from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With whomsoever thou findest thygods, he shall not live. Before our brethren discern what is thine with me, and take [it] to thee. But Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban went into Jacob's tent, and into Leah's tent, and into the two handmaids' tents, and found nothing; and he went out of Leah's tent, and entered into Rachel's tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Rachel had taken the teraphim and put them under the camel's saddle; and she sat upon them. And Laban explored all the tent, but found nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to her father, Let it not be an occasion of anger in the eyes of my lord that I cannot rise up before thee, for it is with me after the manner of women. And he searched carefully, but did not find the teraphim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
