translations.text: And Jacob was angry, and he disputed with Laban. And Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my fault, what my sin, that thou hast so hotly pursued after me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whereas thou hast explored all my baggage, what hast thou found of all thy household stuff? Set [it] here before my brethren and thy brethren, and let them decide between us both.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These twenty years have I been with thee: thy ewes and thy she-goats have not cast their young, and the rams of thy flock I have not eaten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What was torn I have not brought to thee; I had to bear the loss of it: of my hand hast thou required it, [whether] stolen by day or stolen by night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus it was with me: in the day the heat consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep fled from mine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have been these twenty years in thy house: I have served thee fourteen years for thy two daughters, and six years for thy flock; and thou hast changed my wages ten times.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Had not theGod of my father, theGod of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, been with me, it is certain thou wouldest have sent me away now empty.God has looked upon my affliction and the labour of my hands, and has judged last night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban answered and said to Jacob, The daughters are my daughters, and the sons are my sons, and the flock is my flock, and all that thou seest is mine; but as for my daughters, what can I do this day to them, or to their sons whom they have brought forth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, come, let us make a covenant, I and thou; and let it be a witness between me and thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up [for] a pillar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to his brethren, Gather stones. And they took stones, and made a heap, and ate there upon the heap.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, and Jacob called it Galeed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said, This heap is a witness between me and thee this day. Therefore was the name of it called Galeed,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — and Mizpah; for he said, Let Jehovah watch between me and thee, when we shall be hidden one from another:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if thou shouldest afflict my daughters, or if thou shouldest take wives besides my daughters, — no man is with us; see,God is witness between me and thee!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban said to Jacob, Behold this heap, and behold the pillar which I have set up between me and thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [let] this heap be witness, and the pillar a witness, that neither I pass this heap [to go] to thee, nor thou pass this heap and this pillar [to come] to me, for harm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod of Abraham, and theGod of Nahor, theGod of their father, judge between us! And Jacob swore by the fear of his father Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob offered a sacrifice upon the mountain, and invited his brethren to eat bread: and they ate bread, and lodged on the mountain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Laban rose early in the morning, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them; and Laban went and returned to his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob went on his way; and the angels ofGod met him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jacob saw them he said, This is the camp ofGod. And he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob sent messengers before his face to Esau his brother, into the land of Seir, the fields of Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall ye speak to my lord, to Esau: Thy servant Jacob speaks thus — With Laban have I sojourned and tarried until now;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I have oxen, and asses, sheep, and bondmen, and bondwomen; and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find favour in thine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We came to thy brother, to Esau; and he also is coming to meet thee, and four hundred men with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jacob was greatly afraid, and was distressed; and he divided the people that were with him, and the sheep and the cattle and the camels, into two troops.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, If Esau come to the one troop and smite it, then the other troop which is left shall escape.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said,God of my father Abraham, andGod of my father Isaac, Jehovah, who saidst unto me: Return into thy country and to thy kindred, and I will do thee good,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — I am too small for all the loving-kindness and all the faithfulness that thou hast shewn unto thy servant; for with my staff I passed over this Jordan, and now I am become two troops.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau; for I fear him, lest he come and smite me, [and] the mother with the children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou saidst, I will certainly deal well with thee, and make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he lodged there that night; and took of what came to his hand a gift for Esau his brother —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: two hundred she-goats, and twenty he-goats; two hundred ewes, and twenty rams;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thirty milch camels with their colts; forty kine, and ten bulls; twenty she-asses, and ten young asses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he delivered [them] into the hand of his servants, every drove by itself; and he said to his servants, Go on before me, and put a space between drove and drove.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded the foremost, saying, When Esau my brother meets thee, and asks thee, saying, Whose art thou, and where goest thou, and whose are these before thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — then thou shalt say, Thy servant Jacob's: it is a gift sent to my lord, to Esau. And behold, he also is behind us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And so commanded he the second, and the third, and all that followed the droves, saying, According to this word shall ye speak to Esau when ye find him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, moreover, ye shall say, Behold, thy servant Jacob is behind us. For he said, I will propitiate him with the gift that goes before me, and afterwards I will see his face: perhaps he will accept me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the gift went over before him; and he himself lodged that night in the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two maidservants, and his eleven sons, and passed over the ford of the Jabbok;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he took them and led them over the river, and led over what he had.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob remained alone; and a man wrestled with him until the rising of the dawn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he saw that he did not prevail against him, he touched the joint of his thigh; and the joint of Jacob's thigh was dislocated as he wrestled with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Let me go, for the dawn ariseth. And he said, I will not let thee go except thou bless me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, What is thy name? And he said, Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Thy name shall not henceforth be called Jacob, but Israel; for thou hast wrestled withGod, and with men, and hast prevailed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob asked and said, Tell [me], I pray thee, thy name. And he said, How is it that thou askest after my name? And he blessed him there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel — For I have seenGod face to face, and my life has been preserved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he passed over Peniel, the sun rose upon him; and he limped upon his hip.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore the children of Israel do not eat of the sinew that is over the joint of the thigh, to this day; because he touched the joint of Jacob's thigh — the sinew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, Esau was coming, and with him four hundred men. And he distributed the children to Leah, and to Rachel, and to the two maidservants:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he put the maidservants and their children foremost, and Leah and her children after, and Rachel and Joseph hindmost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he passed on before them, and bowed to the earth seven times, until he came near to his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck, and kissed him; and they wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he lifted up his eyes and saw the women and the children, and said, Who are these with thee? And he said, The children thatGod has graciously given thy servant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the maidservants drew near, they and their children, and they bowed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Leah also, with her children, drew near, and they bowed. And lastly Joseph drew near, and Rachel, and they bowed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, What [meanest] thou by all the drove which I met? And he said, To find favour in the eyes of my lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau said, I have enough, my brother; let what thou hast be thine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said, No, I pray thee; if now I have found favour in thine eyes, then receive my gift from my hand; for therefore have I seen thy face, as though I had seen the face ofGod, and thou hast received me with pleasure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take, I pray thee, my blessing which has been brought to thee; becauseGod has been gracious to me, and because I have everything. And he urged him, and he took [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Let us take our journey, and go on, and I will go before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, My lord knows that the children are tender, and the suckling sheep and kine are with me; and if they should overdrive them only one day, all the flock would die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let my lord, I pray thee, pass on before his servant, and I will drive on at my ease according to the pace of the cattle that is before me, and according to the pace of the children, until I come to my lord, to Seir.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau said, Let me now leave with thee [some] of the people that are with me. And he said, What need? Let me find favour in the eyes of my lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house, and for his cattle he made booths. Therefore the name of the place was called Succoth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob came safely [to the] city Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Padan-Aram; and he encamped before the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he bought the portion of the field where he had spread his tent, of the hand of the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, for a hundred kesitahs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there he set up an altar, and called it El-Elohe-Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Dinah the daughter of Leah, whom she had borne to Jacob, went out to see the daughters of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Shechem, the son of Hamor the Hivite, the prince of the country, saw her, he took her, and lay with her, and humbled her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his soul fastened on Dinah the daughter of Jacob, and he loved the maiden, and spoke consolingly to the maiden.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shechem spoke to his father Hamor, saying, Take me this girl as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob heard that he had defiled Dinah his daughter; but his sons were with his cattle in the fields, and Jacob said nothing until they came.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hamor the father of Shechem came out to Jacob, to speak to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Jacob came from the fields when they heard [it]; and the men were grieved, and they were very angry, because he had wrought what was disgraceful in Israel, in lying with Jacob's daughter, which thing ought not to be done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hamor spoke to them, saying, My son Shechem's soul cleaves to your daughter: I pray you, give her to him as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And make marriages with us: give your daughters to us, and take our daughters to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And dwell with us, and the land shall be before you: dwell and trade in it, and get yourselves possessions in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shechem said to her father and to her brethren, Let me find favour in your eyes; and what ye shall say to me I will give.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Impose on me very much as dowry and gift, and I will give according as ye shall say to me; but give me the maiden as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father deceitfully, and spoke — because he had defiled Dinah their sister —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to them, We cannot do this, to give our sister to one that is uncircumcised; for that [were] a reproach to us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But only in this will we consent to you, if ye will be as we, that every male of you be circumcised;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then will we give our daughters to you, and take your daughters to us, and we will dwell with you, and be one people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye do not hearken to us, to be circumcised, then will we take our daughter and go away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their words were good in the eyes of Hamor and Shechem, Hamor's son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the youth did not delay to do this, because he had delight in Jacob's daughter. And he was honourable above all in the house of his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hamor and Shechem his son came to the gate of their city, and spoke to the men of their city, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These men are peaceable with us; therefore let them dwell in the land, and trade in it. And the land — behold, it is of wide extent before them. We will take their daughters as wives, and give them our daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But only in this will the men consent to us to dwell with us, to be one people — if every male among us be circumcised, just as they are circumcised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their cattle, and their possessions, and every beast of theirs, shall they not be ours? only let us consent to them, and they will dwell with us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all that went out at the gate of his city hearkened to Hamor and to Shechem his son; and every male was circumcised — all that went out at the gate of his city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the third day, when they were sore, that two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brethren, took each his sword, and came upon the city boldly, and slew all the males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hamor and Shechem his son they slew with the edge of the sword; and took Dinah out of Shechem's house; and went out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Jacob came upon the slain, and plundered the city, because they had defiled their sister.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their sheep, and their oxen, and their asses, and what [was] in the city, and what [was] in the field they took;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all their goods, and all their little ones, and their wives took they captive, and plundered them, and all that was in the houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, Ye have troubled me, in that ye make me odious among the inhabitants of the land — among the Canaanites and the Perizzites; and I am few men in number, and they will gather themselves against me and smite me, and I shall be destroyed, I and my house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Should people deal with our sister as with a harlot?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said to Jacob, Arise, go up to Bethel, and dwell there, and make there an altar unto theGod that appeared unto thee when thou fleddest from the face of Esau thy brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to his household, and to all that were with him, Put away the strangegods that are among you, and cleanse yourselves, and change your garments;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and we will arise, and go up to Bethel; and I will make there an altar to theGod that answered me in the day of my distress, and was with me in the way that I went.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave to Jacob all the strangegods that were in their hand, and the rings that were in their ears, and Jacob hid them under the terebinth that [is] by Shechem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they journeyed; and the terror ofGod was upon the cities that were round about them, and they did not pursue after the sons of Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob came to Luz, which is in the land of Canaan, that is, Bethel, he and all the people that were with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built there an altar, and called the place El-beth-el; because thereGod had appeared to him when he fled from the face of his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Deborah, Rebecca's nurse, died; and she was buried beneath Bethel, under the oak; and the name of it was called Allon-bachuth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod appeared to Jacob again after he had come from Padan-Aram, and blessed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said to him, Thy name is Jacob: thy name shall not henceforth be called Jacob, but Israel shall be thy name. And he called his name Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said to him, I am the AlmightyGod: be fruitful and multiply; a nation and a company of nations shall be of thee; and kings shall come out of thy loins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the land that I gave Abraham and Isaac, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed after thee will I give the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod went up from him in the place where he had talked with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he had talked with him, a pillar of stone, and poured on it a drink-offering, and poured oil on it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob called the name of the place whereGod had talked with him, Beth-el.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they journeyed from Bethel. And there was yet a certain distance to come to Ephrath, when Rachel travailed in childbirth; and it went hard with her in her childbearing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when it went hard with her in her childbearing, that the midwife said to her, Fear not; for this also is a son for thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as her soul was departing — for she died — that she called his name Benoni; but his father called him Benjamin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rachel died, and was buried on the way to Ephrath, which [is] Bethlehem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob erected a pillar upon her grave: that is the pillar of Rachel's grave to [this] day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel journeyed, and spread his tent on the other side of Migdal-Eder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Israel dwelt in that land, that Reuben went and lay with Bilhah, his father's concubine; and Israel heard of it. And the sons of Jacob were twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Leah: Reuben — Jacob's firstborn — and Simeon, and Levi, and Judah, and Issachar, and Zebulun.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Bilhah, Rachel's maidservant: Dan and Naphtali.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Zilpah, Leah's maidservant: Gad and Asher. These are the sons of Jacob that were born to him in Padan-Aram.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob came to Isaac his father to Mamre — to Kirjath-Arba, which is Hebron; where Abraham had sojourned, and Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the days of Isaac were a hundred and eighty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Isaac expired and died, and was gathered to his peoples, old and full of days. And his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the generations of Esau, that is Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Esau took his wives of the daughters of Canaan: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, daughter of Zibeon the Hivite,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Basmath Ishmael's daughter, the sister of Nebaioth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adah bore to Esau Eliphaz; and Basmath bore Reuel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Oholibamah bore Jeush and Jaalam and Korah. These are the sons of Esau that were born to him in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Esau took his wives, and his sons, and his daughters, and all the souls of his house, and his cattle, and all his beasts, and all his possessions, that he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and went into a country away from his brother Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For their property was too great for them to dwell together, and the land where they were sojourners could not bear them, because of their cattle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus Esau dwelt in mount Seir; Esau is Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the generations of Esau, the father of Edom, in mount Seir.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the names of Esau's sons: Eliphaz, the son of Adah the wife of Esau; Reuel, the son of Basmath the wife of Esau.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Timna was concubine to Eliphaz Esau's son, and she bore Amalek to Eliphaz. These are the sons of Adah Esau's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And these are the sons of Reuel: Nahath and Zerah, Shammah and Mizzah. These are the sons of Basmath Esau's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And these are the sons of Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah, daughter of Zibeon, Esau's wife: and she bore to Esau Jeush and Jaalam and Korah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the chiefs of the sons of Esau. The sons of Eliphaz, the firstborn of Esau: chief Teman, chief Omar, chief Zepho, chief Kenaz,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: chief Korah, chief Gatam, chief Amalek. These are the chiefs of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Adah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the sons of Reuel Esau's son: chief Nahath, chief Zerah, chief Shammah, chief Mizzah: these are the chiefs of Reuel in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Basmath Esau's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And these are the sons of Oholibamah Esau's wife: chief Jeush, chief Jaalam, chief Korah; these are the chiefs of Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, Esau's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the sons of Esau, and these their chiefs: he is Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitant of the land: Lotan, and Shobal, and Zibeon, and Anah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Dishon, and Ezer, and Dishan. These are the chiefs of the Horites, the sons of Seir in the land of Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Lotan were Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And these are the sons of Shobal: Alvan, and Manahath, and Ebal, Shepho and Onam.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And these are the sons of Zibeon: both Ajah and Anah. This is the Anah that found the warm springs in the wilderness as he fed the asses of Zibeon his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And these are the sons of Anah: Dishon, and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And these are the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, and Eshban, and Ithran, and Cheran.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, and Zaavan, and Akan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the chiefs of the Horites: chief Lotan, chief Shobal, chief Zibeon, chief Anah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: chief Dishon, chief Ezer, chief Dishan. These are the chiefs of the Horites, according to their chiefs in the land of Seir.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the kings that reigned in the land of Edom before there reigned a king over the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom; and the name of his city was Dinhabah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bela died; and Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jobab died; and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Husham died; and Hadad the son of Bedad, who smote Midian in the fields of Moab, reigned in his stead. And the name of his city was Avith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hadad died; and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samlah died; and Saul of Rehoboth on the river reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul died; and Baal-hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baal-hanan the son of Achbor died; and Hadar reigned in his stead; and the name of his city was Pau; and his wife's name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred daughter of Mezahab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the chiefs of Esau, according to their families, after their places, with their names: chief Timna, chief Alvah, chief Jetheth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: chief Oholibamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mibzar,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: chief Magdiel, chief Iram. These are the chiefs of Edom, according to their dwelling-places in the land of their possession. This is Esau, the father of Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob dwelt in the land where his father sojourned — in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, fed the flock with his brethren; and he was doing service with the sons of Bilhah, and with the sons of Zilpah, his father's wives. And Joseph brought to his father an evil report of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel loved Joseph more than all his sons, because he was son of his old age; and he made him a vest of many colours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren saw that their father loved him more than all his brethren, and they hated him, and could not greet him with friendliness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph dreamed a dream, and told [it] to his brethren, and they hated him yet the more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Hear, I pray you, this dream, which I have dreamt:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, we were binding sheaves in the fields, and lo, my sheaf rose up, and remained standing; and behold, your sheaves came round about and bowed down to my sheaf.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren said to him, Wilt thou indeed be a king over us? wilt thou indeed rule over us? And they hated him yet the more for his dreams and for his words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he dreamed yet another dream, and told it to his brethren, and said, Behold, I have dreamt another dream, and behold, the sun and the moon and eleven stars bowed down to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he told [it] to his father and to his brethren. And his father rebuked him, and said to him, What is this dream which thou hast dreamt? Shall we indeed come, I and thy mother and thy brethren, to bow down ourselves to thee to the earth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren envied him; but his father kept the saying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren went to feed their father's flock at Shechem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel said to Joseph, Do not thy brethren feed [the flock] at Shechem? Come, that I may send thee to them. And he said to him, Here am I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Go, I pray thee, see after the welfare of thy brethren, and after the welfare of the flock; and bring me word again. And he sent him out of the vale of Hebron; and he came towards Shechem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a man found him, and behold, he was wandering in the country; and the man asked him, saying, What seekest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I am seeking my brethren: tell me, I pray thee, where they feed [their flocks].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man said, They have removed from this; for I heard them say, Let us go to Dothan. And Joseph went after his brethren, and found them at Dothan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they saw him from afar, and before he came near to them, they conspired against him to put him to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said one to another, Behold, there comes that dreamer!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now come and let us kill him, and cast him into one of the pits, and we will say, An evil beast has devoured him; and we will see what becomes of his dreams.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Reuben heard [it], and delivered him out of their hand, and said, Let us not take his life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Reuben said to them, Shed no blood: cast him into this pit which is in the wilderness; but lay no hand upon him — in order that he might deliver him out of their hand, to bring him to his father again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Joseph came to his brethren, that they stripped Joseph of his vest, the vest of many colours, which he had on;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they took him and cast him into the pit; now the pit was empty — there was no water in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sat down to eat bread; and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and behold, a caravan of Ishmaelites came from Gilead; and their camels bore tragacanth, and balsam, and ladanum — going to carry [it] down to Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah said to his brethren, What profit is it that we kill our brother and secrete his blood?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites; but let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother, our flesh. And his brethren hearkened [to him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Midianitish men, merchants, passed by; and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty silver-pieces; and they brought Joseph to Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Reuben returned to the pit, and behold, Joseph [was] not in the pit; and he rent his garments,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and returned to his brethren, and said, The child is not; and I, where shall I go?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took Joseph's vest, and slaughtered a buck of the goats, and dipped the vest in the blood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they sent the vest of many colours and had it carried to their father, and said, This have we found: discern now whether it is thy son's vest or not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he discerned it, and said, [It is] my son's vest! an evil beast has devoured him: Joseph is without doubt rent in pieces!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob rent his clothes, and put sackcloth on his loins, and mourned for his son many days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted, and said, For I will go down to my son into Sheol mourning. Thus his father wept for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Midianites sold him into Egypt, to Potiphar, a chamberlain of Pharaoh, the captain of the life-guard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at that time, that Judah went down from his brethren, and turned in to a man of Adullam whose name was Hirah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah saw there the daughter of a Canaanitish man whose name was Shua; and he took her, and went in to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she conceived and bore a son; and he called his name Er.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she again conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Onan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again she bore a son, and she called his name Shelah; and he was at Chezib when she bore him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Er, Judah's firstborn, was wicked in the sight of Jehovah, and Jehovah slew him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Judah said to Onan, Go in to thy brother's wife, and fulfil to her the brother-in-law's duty, and raise up seed to thy brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when Onan knew that the seed should not be his own, it came to pass when he went in to his brother's wife, that he spilled [it] on the ground, in order to give no seed to his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the thing which he did was evil in the sight of Jehovah, and he slew him also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, Remain a widow in thy father's house, until Shelah my son is grown; for he said, Lest he die also, as his brethren. And Tamar went and remained in her father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as the days were multiplied, Judah's wife, the daughter of Shua, died. And Judah was comforted, and he went up to his sheep-shearers, to Timnah, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Tamar, saying, Behold thy father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she put the garments of her widowhood off from her, and covered herself with a veil, and wrapped herself, and sat in the entry of Enaim, which is on the way to Timnah; for she saw that Shelah was grown, and she was not given to him as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah saw her, and took her for a harlot; because she had covered her face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he turned aside to her by the way, and said, Come, I pray thee, let me go in to thee; for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. And she said, What wilt thou give me, that thou mayest come in to me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I will send [thee] a kid of the goats from the flock. And she said, Wilt thou give [me] a pledge, until thou send it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, What pledge shall I give thee? And she said, Thy signet, and thy lace, and thy staff which is in thy hand. And he gave [it] her, and went in to her; and she conceived by him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she arose and went away; and she laid by her veil from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah sent the kid of the goats by the hand of his friend the Adullamite, to receive the pledge from the woman's hand; but he found her not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked the men of her place, saying, Where is the prostitute that was at Enaim, by the way-side? And they said, There was no prostitute here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he returned to Judah, and said, I have not found her; and also the men of the place said, No prostitute has been here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Judah said, Let her take [it] for herself, lest we be put to shame. Behold, I sent this kid, and thou hast not found her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass about three months after, that it was told Judah, saying, Tamar thy daughter-in-law has committed fornication, and behold, she is also with child by fornication. And Judah said, Bring her forth, that she may be burned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When she was brought forth, she sent to her father-in-law, saying, By the man to whom these [belong] am I with child; and she said, Acknowledge, I pray thee, whose are this signet, and this lace, and this staff.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah acknowledged [them], and said, She is more righteous than I, because I have not given her to Shelah my son. And he knew her again no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the time of her delivery, that behold, twins were in her womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when she brought forth, that one stretched out [his] hand, and the midwife took it and bound round his hand a scarlet thread, saying, This came out first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he drew back his hand, that behold, his brother came out; and she said, How hast thou broken forth! on thee be the breach! And they called his name Pherez.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And afterwards came out his brother, round whose hand was the scarlet thread; and they called his name Zerah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph was brought down to Egypt; and Potiphar, a chamberlain of Pharaoh, the captain of the life-guard, an Egyptian, bought him of the hand of the Ishmaelites who had brought him down thither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah was with Joseph, and he was a prosperous man; and he was in the house of his master the Egyptian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his master saw that Jehovah was with him, and that Jehovah made all that he did to prosper in his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph found favour in his eyes, and attended on him; and he set him over his house, and all that he had he gave into his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass from the time he had set him over his house and all that he had, that Jehovah blessed the Egyptian's house for Joseph's sake; and the blessing of Jehovah was on all that he had in the house and in the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he left all that he had in Joseph's hand, and took cognizance of nothing with him, save the bread that he ate. And Joseph was of a beautiful form and of a beautiful countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these things, that his master's wife cast her eyes on Joseph, and said, Lie with me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he refused, and said to his master's wife, Behold, my master takes cognizance of nothing with me: what is in the house, and all that he has, he has given into my hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is none greater in this house than I; neither has he withheld anything from me but thee, because thou art his wife; and how should I do this great wickedness, and sin againstGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as she spoke to Joseph day by day and he hearkened not to her, to lie with her [and] to be with her,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that on a certain day he went into the house to do his business, and there was none of the men of the house there in the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then she caught him by his garment, saying, Lie with me! But he left his garment in her hand, and fled and ran out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and had fled forth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that she called to the men of her house, and spoke to them, saying, See, he has brought in a Hebrew man to us, to mock us: he came in to me, to lie with me; and I cried with a loud voice;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it came to pass when he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment with me, and fled and went out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she laid his garment by her until his lord came home.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she spoke to him according to these words, saying, The Hebrew bondman that thou hast brought to us came in to me to mock me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it came to pass as I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment with me, and fled forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when his lord heard the words of his wife which she spoke to him, saying, After this manner did thy bondman to me, that his wrath was kindled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph's lord took him and put him into the tower-house, [the] place where the king's prisoners were confined; and he was there in the tower-house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah was with Joseph, and extended mercy to him, and gave him favour in the eyes of the chief of the tower-house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief of the tower-house committed to Joseph's hand all the prisoners that were in the tower-house; and whatever they had to do there he did.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The chief of the tower-house looked not to anything under his hand, because Jehovah was with him; and what he did, Jehovah made it prosper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these things, [that] the cup-bearer of the king of Egypt and the baker offended their lord the king of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh was wroth with his two chamberlains — with the chief of the cup-bearers and with the chief of the bakers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he put them in custody into the house of the captain of the life-guard, into the tower-house, into the place where Joseph was imprisoned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the captain of the life-guard appointed Joseph to them, that he should attend on them. And they were [several] days in custody.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they dreamed a dream, both of them in one night, each his dream, each according to the interpretation of his dream, the cup-bearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were imprisoned in the tower-house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph came in to them in the morning, and looked on them, and behold, they were sad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked Pharaoh's chamberlains that were with him in custody in his lord's house, saying, Why are your faces [so] sad to-day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, We have dreamt a dream, and there is no interpreter of it. And Joseph said to them, [Do] not interpretations [belong] toGod? tell me [your dreams], I pray you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the chief of the cup-bearers told his dream to Joseph, and said to him, In my dream, behold, a vine was before me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in the vine were three branches; and it was as though it budded: its blossoms shot forth, its clusters ripened into grapes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh's cup was in my hand; and I took the grapes, and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup, and gave the cup into Pharaoh's hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to him, This is the interpretation of it: the three branches are three days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In yet three days will Pharaoh lift up thy head and restore thee to thy place, and thou shalt deliver Pharaoh's cup into his hand, after the former manner when thou wast his cup-bearer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only bear a remembrance with thee of me when it goes well with thee, and deal kindly, I pray thee, with me, and make mention of me to Pharaoh, and bring me out of this house;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for indeed I was stolen out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also have I done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the chief of the bakers saw that the interpretation was good, he said to Joseph, I also was in my dream, and behold, three baskets of white bread were on my head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the uppermost basket there were all manner of victuals for Pharaoh that the baker makes, and the birds ate them out of the basket upon my head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph answered and said, This is the interpretation of it: the three baskets are three days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In yet three days will Pharaoh lift up thy head from off thee, and hang thee on a tree; and the birds will eat thy flesh from off thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the third day — Pharaoh's birthday — that he made a feast to all his bondmen. And he lifted up the head of the chief of the cup-bearers, and the head of the chief of the bakers among his bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he restored the chief of the cup-bearers to his office of cup-bearer again; and he gave the cup into Pharaoh's hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hanged the chief of the bakers, as Joseph had interpreted to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the chief of the cup-bearers did not remember Joseph, and forgot him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of two full years, that Pharaoh dreamed, and behold, he stood by the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there came up out of the river seven kine, fine-looking and fat-fleshed, and they fed in the reed-grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, seven other kine came up after them out of the river, bad-looking and lean-fleshed, and stood by the kine on the bank of the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the kine that were bad-looking and lean-fleshed ate up the seven kine that were fine-looking and fat. And Pharaoh awoke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he slept and dreamed the second time; and behold, seven ears of corn grew up on one stalk, fat and good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, seven ears, thin and parched with the east wind, sprung up after them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the thin ears devoured the seven fat and full ears. And Pharaoh awoke; and behold, it was a dream.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the morning, that his spirit was troubled; and he sent and called for all the scribes of Egypt, and all the sages who were therein, and Pharaoh told them his dream; but [there was] none to interpret them to Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then spoke the chief of the cup-bearers to Pharaoh, saying, I remember mine offences this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh was wroth with his bondmen, and put me in custody into the captain of the life-guard's house, me and the chief of the bakers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we dreamed a dream in one night, I and he; we dreamed each according to the interpretation of his dream.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was there with us a Hebrew youth, a bondman of the captain of the life-guard, to whom we told [them], and he interpreted to us our dreams; to each he interpreted according to his dream.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, just as he interpreted to us, so it came about: me has he restored to my office, and him he hanged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph; and they brought him hastily out of the dungeon. And he shaved [himself], and changed his clothes, and came in to Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, I have dreamt a dream, and there is none to interpret it. And I have heard say of thee, thou understandest a dream to interpret it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, It is not in me:God will give Pharaoh an answer of peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, In my dream, behold, I stood on the bank of the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there came up out of the river seven kine, fat-fleshed and of fine form, and they fed in the reed-grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, seven other kine came up after them, poor, and very ill-formed, and lean-fleshed — such as I never saw in all the land of Egypt for badness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lean and bad kine ate up the seven first fat kine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they came into their belly, and it could not be known that they had come into their belly; and their look was bad, as at the beginning. And I awoke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I saw in my dream, and behold, seven ears came up on one stalk, full and good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, seven ears, withered, thin, parched with the east wind, sprung up after them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the thin ears devoured the seven good ears. And I told it to the scribes; but there was none to make it known to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to Pharaoh, The dream of Pharaoh is one. WhatGod will do he has made known to Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The seven fine kine are seven years; and the seven good ears are seven years: the dream is one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the seven lean and bad kine that came up after them are seven years; and the seven empty ears, parched with the east wind, will be seven years of famine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the word which I have spoken to Pharaoh: whatGod is about to do he has let Pharaoh see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, there come seven years of great plenty throughout the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there will arise after them seven years of famine; and all the plenty will be forgotten in the land of Egypt, and the famine will waste away the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the plenty will not be known afterwards in the land by reason of that famine; for it will be very grievous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as regards the double repetition of the dream to Pharaoh, it is that the thing is established byGod, andGod will hasten to do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now let Pharaoh look himself out a man discreet and wise, and set him over the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let Pharaoh do [this]: let him appoint overseers over the land, and take the fifth part of the land of Egypt during the seven years of plenty,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and let them gather all the food of these coming good years, and lay up corn under the hand of Pharaoh, for food in the cities, and keep [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let the food be as store for the land for the seven years of famine, which will be in the land of Egypt, that the land perish not through the famine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word was good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of all his bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to his bondmen, Shall we find [one] as this, a man in whom the Spirit ofGod is?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, SinceGod has made all this known to thee, there is none [so] discreet and wise as thou.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt be over my house, and according to thy commandment shall all my people regulate themselves; only concerning the throne will I be greater than thou.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, See, I have set thee over all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh took off his ring from his hand, and put it on Joseph's hand, and arrayed him in clothes of byssus, and put a gold chain on his neck.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he caused him to ride in the second chariot that he had; and they cried before him, Bow the knee! and he set him over all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, I am Pharaoh; and without thee shall no man lift up his hand or his foot in all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphnath-paaneah, and gave him as wife Asnath the daughter of Potipherah the priest in On. And Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from Pharaoh, and passed through the whole land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the seven years of plenty the land brought forth by handfuls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gathered up all the food of the seven years that was in the land of Egypt, and put the food in the cities; the food of the fields of the city, which were round about it, he laid up in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph laid up corn as sand of the sea exceeding much, until they left off numbering; for it was without number.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to Joseph were born two sons before the year of famine came, whom Asnath the daughter of Potipherah the priest in On bore to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh — ForGod has made me forget all my toil, and all my father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the name of the second he called Ephraim — ForGod has caused me to be fruitful in the land of my affliction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the seven years of plenty that were in the land of Egypt were ended;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the seven years of the dearth began to come, according as Joseph had said. And there was dearth in all lands; but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the land of Egypt suffered from the dearth. And the people cried to Pharaoh for bread; and Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, Go to Joseph: what he says to you, that do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the famine was on all the earth. And Joseph opened every place in which there was [provision], and sold grain to the Egyptians; and the famine was grievous in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all countries came into Egypt to Joseph, to buy [grain], because the famine was grievous on the whole earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt, and Jacob said to his sons, Why do ye look one upon another?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Behold, I have heard that there is grain in Egypt; go down thither and buy [grain] for us from thence, in order that we may live, and not die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph's ten brethren went down to buy [grain] out of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Benjamin, Joseph's brother, Jacob sent not with his brethren; for he said, Lest mischief may befall him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So the sons of Israel came to buy [grain] among those that came; for the famine was in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph, he was the governor over the land — he it was that sold [the corn] to all the people of the land. And Joseph's brethren came and bowed down to him, the face to the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph saw his brethren, and knew them; but he made himself strange to them, and spoke roughly to them, and said to them, Whence come ye? And they said, From the land of Canaan, to buy food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph knew his brethren, but they did not know him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph remembered the dreams that he had dreamt of them; and he said to them, Ye are spies: to see the exposed places of the land ye are come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, No, my lord; but to buy food are thy servants come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We are all one man's sons; we are honest: thy servants are not spies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, No; but to see the exposed places of the land are ye come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Thy servants were twelve brethren, sons of one man, in the land of Canaan; and behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to them, That is it that I have spoken to you, saying, Ye are spies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By this ye shall be put to the proof: as Pharaoh lives, ye shall not go forth hence, unless your youngest brother come hither!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Send one of you, that he may fetch your brother, but ye shall be imprisoned, and your words shall be put to the proof, whether the truth is in you; and if not, as Pharaoh lives, ye are spies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put them in custody three days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to them the third day, This do, that ye may live: I fearGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye are honest, let one of your brethren remain bound in the house of your prison, but go ye, carry grain for the hunger of your households;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and bring your youngest brother to me, in order that your words be verified, and that ye may not die. And they did so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they said one to another, We are indeed guilty concerning our brother, whose anguish of soul we saw when he besought us, and we did not hearken; therefore this distress is come upon us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Reuben answered them, saying, Did I not speak to you, saying, Do not sin against the lad? But ye did not hearken; and now behold, his blood also is required.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they did not know that Joseph understood, for the interpreter was between them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he turned away from them, and wept. And he returned to them, and spoke to them, and took Simeon from among them, and bound him before their eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph gave orders to fill their vessels with corn, and to restore every man's money into his sack, and to give them provision for the way. And thus did they to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they loaded their asses with their grain, and departed thence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of them opened his sack to give his ass food in the inn, and saw his money, and behold, it was in the mouth of his sack.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his brethren, My money is returned [to me], and behold, it is even in my sack. And their heart failed [them], and they were afraid, saying one to another, What is this [that]God has done to us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came into the land of Canaan, to Jacob their father, and told him all that had befallen them, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The man, the lord of the land, spoke roughly to us, and treated us as spies of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we said to him, We are honest; we are not spies:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: we are twelve brethren, sons of our father; one is not, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of Canaan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man, the lord of the land, said to us, Hereby shall I know that ye are honest: leave one of your brethren with me, and take [for] the hunger of your households, and go,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and bring your youngest brother to me, and I shall know that ye are not spies, but are honest. Your brother will I give up to you; and ye may trade in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they emptied their sacks, that behold, every man had his bundle of money in his sack; and they saw their bundles of money, they and their father, and were afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob their father said to them, Ye have bereaved me of children: Joseph is not, and Simeon is not, and ye will take Benjamin! All these things are against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Reuben spoke to his father, saying, Slay my two sons if I bring him not back to thee: give him into my hand, and I will bring him to thee again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said, My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he alone is left; and if mischief should befall him by the way in which ye go, then would ye bring down my grey hairs with sorrow to Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the famine was grievous in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when they had finished eating the grain which they had brought from Egypt, that their father said to them, Go again, buy us a little food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah spoke to him, saying, The man did positively testify to us, saying, Ye shall not see my face, unless your brother be with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go down and buy thee food;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if thou do not send [him], we will not go down, for the man said to us, Ye shall not see my face, unless your brother be with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel said, Why did ye deal [so] ill with me [as] to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, The man asked very closely after us, and after our kindred, saying, Is your father yet alive? have ye a brother? And we told him according to the tenor of these words. Could we at all know that he would say, Bring your brother down?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah said to Israel his father, Send the lad with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live, and not die, both we and thou and our little ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will be surety for him: of my hand shalt thou require him; if I bring him not to thee, and set him before thy face, then shall I be guilty toward thee for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For had we not lingered, we should now certainly have returned already twice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their father Israel said to them, If it is then so, do this: take of the best fruits in the land in your vessels, and carry down the man a gift: a little balsam and a little honey, tragacanth and ladanum, pistacia-nuts and almonds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And take other money in your hand, and the money that was returned to you in the mouth of your sacks, carry back in your hand: perhaps it is an oversight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And take your brother, and arise, go again to the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the AlmightyGod give you mercy before the man, that he may send away your other brother and Benjamin! And I, if I be bereaved of children, am bereaved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men took that gift, and took double money in their hand, and Benjamin, and rose up, and went down to Egypt, and came before Joseph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph saw Benjamin with them, and said to the [man] who was over his house, Bring the men into the house, and slaughter cattle, and make ready; for the men shall eat with me at noon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man did as Joseph had said; and the man brought the men into Joseph's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men were afraid because they were brought into Joseph's house, and said, Because of the money that was returned to us in our sacks at the beginning are we brought in, that he may turn against us, and fall upon us and take us for bondmen, and our asses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came up to the man that was over Joseph's house, and they spoke to him at the door of the house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Ah! my lord, we came indeed down at the first to buy food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when we came to the inn, that we opened our sacks, and behold, [every] man's money was in the mouth of his sack, our money according to its weight; and we have brought it again in our hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And other money have we brought down in our hand to buy food. We do not know who put our money in our sacks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Peace be to you, fear not: yourGod, and theGod of your father, has given you treasure in your sacks; your money came to me. And he brought Simeon out to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man brought the men into Joseph's house, and gave water, and they washed their feet; and he gave their asses food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made ready the gift for Joseph's coming at noon; for they had heard that they should eat bread there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Joseph came home, they brought him the gift that was in their hand, into the house, and bowed themselves to him to the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked them of their welfare, and said, Is your father well — the old man of whom ye spoke? Is he yet alive?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Thy servant our father is well; he is yet alive. And they bowed, and made obeisance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he lifted up his eyes and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother's son, and said, Is this your younger brother of whom ye spoke to me? And he said,God be gracious to thee, my son!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph made haste, for his bowels burned for his brother; and he sought [a place] to weep, and he went into the chamber, and wept there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he washed his face, and came out, and controlled himself, and said, Set on bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set on for him by himself, and for them by themselves, and for the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves; because the Egyptians might not eat bread with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination unto the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth; and the men marvelled one at another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had portions carried to them from before him. And Benjamin's portion was five times greater than the portions of them all. And they drank, and made merry with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded him who was over his house, saying, Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry; and put every man's money in the mouth of his sack.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, and his grain-money. And he did according to the word of Joseph which he had spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the morning, when it was light, the men were sent away, they and their asses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were gone out of the city, [and] not [yet] far off, when Joseph said to him who was over his house, Up! follow after the men; and when thou overtakest them, thou shalt say to them, Why have ye rewarded evil for good?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not this [it] in which my lord drinks, and in which indeed he divines? Ye have done evil [in] what ye have done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he overtook them, and he spoke to them these words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from thy servants to do such a thing!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the money that we found in our sacks' mouths we have brought again to thee from the land of Canaan; and how should we steal out of thy lord's house silver or gold?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With whomsoever of thy servants it is found, let him die; and we also will be my lord's bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Now also [let] it [be] according to your words: let him with whom it is found be my bondman, but ye shall be blameless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they hasted and laid down every man his sack on the ground, and opened every man his sack.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he searched carefully: he began at the eldest, and ended at the youngest; and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they rent their clothes, and loaded every man his ass, and they returned to the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah and his brethren came to Joseph's house; and he was still there; and they fell down before him to the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to them, What deed is this which ye have done? Did ye not know that such a man as I can certainly divine?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah said, What shall we say to my lord? what shall we speak, and how justify ourselves?God has found out the iniquity of thy servants; behold, we are my lord's bondmen, both we, and he in whose hand the cup has been found.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Far be it from me to do so! The man in whose hand the cup has been found, he shall be my bondman; but as for you, go up in peace to your father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Judah came near to him, and said, Ah! my lord, let thy servant, I pray thee, speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant; for thou art even as Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My lord asked his servants, saying, Have ye a father, or a brother?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we said to my lord, We have an aged father, and a child born to him in his old age, [yet] young; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother; and his father loves him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Bring him down to me, that I may set mine eye on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we said to my lord, The youth cannot leave his father: if he should leave his father, [his father] would die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou saidst to thy servants, Unless your youngest brother come down with you, ye shall see my face no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when we came up to thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And our father said, Go again, buy us a little food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we said, We cannot go down: if our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down; for we cannot see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thy servant my father said to us, Ye know that my wife bore me two [sons];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the one went out from me, and I said, He must certainly have been torn in pieces; and I have not seen him [again] hitherto.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye take this one also from me, and mischief should befall him, ye will bring down my grey hairs with misery to Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
