translations.text: And now, when I come to thy servant my father, and the lad is not with us, — seeing that his life is bound up with his life,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: it will come to pass when he sees that the lad is not [there], that he will die; and thy servants will bring down the grey hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy servant became surety for the lad to my father, saying, If I bring him not to thee, then I shall be guilty toward my father all my days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, let thy servant stay, I pray thee, instead of the lad a bondman to my lord, and let the lad go up with his brethren;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for how should I go up to my father if the lad were not with me? — lest I see the evil that would come on my father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph could not control himself before all them that stood by him, and he cried, Put every man out from me! And no man stood with him when Joseph made himself known to his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he raised his voice in weeping; and the Egyptians heard, and the house of Pharaoh heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to his brethren, I am Joseph. Does my father yet live? And his brethren could not answer him, for they were troubled at his presence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to his brethren, Come near to me, I pray you. And they came near. And he said, I am Joseph your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, be not grieved, and be not angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither, forGod sent me before you to preserve life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the famine has been these two years in the land; and yet there are five years in which there will be neither ploughing nor harvest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: SoGod sent me before you to preserve you a remnant in the earth, and to save you alive by a great deliverance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now it was not you [that] sent me here, butGod; and he has made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house, and governor over all the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Haste and go up to my father, and say to him, Thus says thy son Joseph:God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me, tarry not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt dwell in the land of Goshen, and thou shalt be near to me, thou, and thy sons, and thy sons' sons, and thy sheep, and thy cattle, and all that thou hast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there will I maintain thee; for yet there are five years of famine; in order that thou be not impoverished, thou, and thy household, and all that thou hast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin, that it is my mouth which speaks to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And tell my father of all my glory in Egypt, and of all that ye have seen, and haste and bring down my father hither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he fell on his brother Benjamin's neck, and wept; and Benjamin wept on his neck.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he kissed all his brethren, and wept upon them; and after that his brethren talked with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the report was heard in Pharaoh's house, saying, Joseph's brethren are come. And it was good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, Say to thy brethren, Do this: load your beasts and depart, go into the land of Canaan,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and take your father and your households, and come to me; and I will give you the good of the land of Egypt, and ye shall eat the fat of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou art commanded — this do: take waggons out of the land of Egypt for your little ones and for your wives, and take up your father, and come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let not your eye regret your stuff; for the good of all the land of Egypt shall be yours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Israel did so; and Joseph gave them waggons, according to the commandment of Pharaoh, and gave them provision for the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To each one of them all he gave changes of clothing; but to Benjamin he gave three hundred [pieces] of silver and five changes of clothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to his father he sent this: ten asses laden with the good things of Egypt, and ten she-asses laden with corn and bread, and food for his father by the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent his brethren away, and they departed. And he said to them, Do not quarrel on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went up out of Egypt, and came into the land of Canaan to Jacob their father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they told him, saying, Joseph is still alive, and he is governor over all the land of Egypt. And his heart fainted, for he did not believe them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they spoke to him all the words of Joseph, which he had spoken to them. And he saw the waggons that Joseph had sent to carry him. And the spirit of Jacob their father revived.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel said, It is enough: Joseph my son is yet alive; I will go and see him before I die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel took his journey with all that he had, and came to Beer-sheba; and he offered sacrifices to theGod of his father Isaac.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod spoke to Israel in the visions of the night and said, Jacob, Jacob! And he said, Here am I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I amGod, theGod of thy father: fear not to go down to Egypt; for I will there make of thee a great nation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will go down with thee to Egypt, and I will also certainly bring thee up; and Joseph shall put his hand on thine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob rose up from Beer-sheba; and the sons of Israel carried Jacob their father, and their little ones, and their wives, on the waggons that Pharaoh had sent to carry him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took their cattle, and their goods which they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and came to Egypt, Jacob and all his seed with him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: his sons and his sons' sons with him, his daughters and his sons' daughters and all his seed he brought with him to Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the sons of Israel who came into Egypt: Jacob and his sons. Jacob's firstborn, Reuben.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Reuben: Enoch, and Phallu, and Hezron, and Carmi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Simeon: Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Saul the son of a Canaanitish woman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Judah: Er, and Onan, and Shelah, and Pherez, and Zerah; but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan. And the sons of Pherez were Hezron and Hamul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Issachar: Tola, and Puah, and Job, and Shimron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Zebulun: Sered, and Elon, and Jahleel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — These are the sons of Leah, whom she bore to Jacob in Padan-Aram; and his daughter, Dinah. All the souls of his sons and his daughters were thirty-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Gad: Ziphion and Haggi, Shuni and Ezbon, Eri, and Arodi, and Areli.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Asher: Jimnah, and Jishvah, and Jishvi, and Beriah; and Serah their sister; and the sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — These are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to Leah his daughter; and she bore these to Jacob: sixteen souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Rachel Jacob's wife: Joseph and Benjamin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to Joseph in the land of Egypt were born Manasseh and Ephraim, whom Asnath bore to him, the daughter of Potipherah the priest in On.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Benjamin: Belah, and Becher, and Ashbel, Gera and Naaman, Ehi and Rosh, Muppim, and Huppim, and Ard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — These are the sons of Rachel who were born to Jacob: all the souls were fourteen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Dan: Hushim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Naphtali: Jahzeel, and Guni, and Jezer, and Shillem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — These are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter; and she bore these to Jacob: all the souls were seven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the souls that came with Jacob to Egypt, that had come out of his loins, besides Jacob's sons' wives: all the souls were sixty-six.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Joseph who were born to him in Egypt, were two souls. All the souls of the house of Jacob that came to Egypt were seventy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent Judah before him to Joseph, to give notice before he came to Goshen. And they came into the land of Goshen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Joseph yoked his chariot, and went up to meet Israel his father, to Goshen, and he presented himself to him; and he fell on his neck, and wept on his neck a good while.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel said to Joseph, Now let me die, after I have seen thy face, since thou still livest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to his brethren and to his father's house, I will go up, and tell Pharaoh, and say to him, My brethren and my father's house, who were in the land of Canaan, are come to me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the men are shepherds, for they have been occupied with cattle; and they have brought their sheep, and their cattle, and all that they have.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass that when Pharaoh shall call you and say, What is your occupation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then ye shall say, Thy servants are men that have been occupied with cattle from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers; in order that ye may dwell in the land of Goshen; for every shepherd is an abomination to the Egyptians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph came and told Pharaoh and said, My father and my brethren, and their sheep and their cattle, and all that they have, are come out of the land of Canaan; and behold, they are in the land of Goshen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took from the whole number of his brethren, five men, and set them before Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to his brethren, What is your occupation? And they said to Pharaoh, Thy servants are shepherds, both we and our fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to Pharaoh, To sojourn in the land are we come; for there is no pasture for the sheep that thy servants have, for the famine is grievous in the land of Canaan; and now, we pray thee, let thy servants dwell in the land of Goshen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh spoke to Joseph, saying, Thy father and thy brethren are come to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The land of Egypt is before thee; in the best of the land settle thy father and thy brethren: let them dwell in the land of Goshen. And if thou knowest men of activity among them, then set them as overseers of cattle over what I have.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph brought Jacob his father, and set him before Pharaoh. And Jacob blessed Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to Jacob, How many are the days of the years of thy life?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to Pharaoh, The days of the years of my sojourning are a hundred and thirty years. Few and evil have been the days of the years of my life, and they do not attain to the days of the years of the life of my fathers, in the days of their sojourning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob blessed Pharaoh, and went out from Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph settled his father and his brethren, and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph maintained his father, and his brethren, and all his father's household, with bread, according to the number of the little ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was no bread in all the land; for the famine was very grievous; and the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan were exhausted through the famine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph gathered up all the money that was found in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, for the grain which they bought; and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when money came to an end in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, all the Egyptians came to Joseph, saying, Give us bread! for why should we die before thee? for [our] money is all gone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said, Give your cattle, and I will give you for your cattle, if [your] money be all gone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought their cattle to Joseph; and Joseph gave them bread for horses, and for flocks of sheep, and for herds of cattle, and for asses; and he fed them with bread for all their cattle that year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that year ended; and they came to him the second year, and said to him, We will not hide [it] from my lord that since [our] money is come to an end, and the herds of cattle are in the possession of my lord, nothing is left before my lord but our bodies and our land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why should we die before thine eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land for bread, and we and our land will be bondmen to Pharaoh; and give seed, that we may live, and not die, and that the land be not desolate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph bought all the soil of Egypt for Pharaoh; for the Egyptians sold every man his field, because the famine prevailed over them; and the land became Pharaoh's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as for the people, he removed them into the cities, from [one] end of the borders of Egypt even to the [other] end of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only the land of the priests he did not buy; for the priests had an assigned portion from Pharaoh, and ate their assigned portion which Pharaoh had given them; so they did not sell their land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to the people, Behold, I have bought you this day and your land for Pharaoh: lo, here is seed for you, and sow the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass in the increase that ye shall give the fifth to Pharaoh, and the four parts shall be your own, for seed of the field, and for your food, and for them of your households, and for food for your little ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Thou hast saved us alive. Let us find favour in the eyes of my lord, and we will be Pharaoh's bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph made it a law over the land of Egypt to this day, [that] the fifth should be for Pharaoh, except the land of the priests: theirs alone did not become Pharaoh's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen; and they had possessions in it, and were fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years; and the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were a hundred and forty-seven years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the days of Israel approached that he should die. And he called his son Joseph, and said to him, If now I have found favour in thine eyes, put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh, and deal kindly and truly with me: bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but when I shall lie with my fathers, thou shalt carry me out of Egypt, and bury me in their sepulchre. And he said, I will do according to thy word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Swear to me; and he swore to him. And Israel worshipped on the bed's head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these things, that one told Joseph, Behold, thy father is sick. And he took with him his two sons Manasseh and Ephraim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one told Jacob and said, Behold, thy son Joseph is coming to thee. And Israel strengthened himself, and sat upon the bed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob said to Joseph, The AlmightyGod appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and blessed me,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he said to me, Behold, I will make thee fruitful and multiply thee, and I will make of thee a company of peoples; and will give this land to thy seed after thee [for] an everlasting possession.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now thy two sons, who were born to thee in the land of Egypt before I came to thee into Egypt, shall be mine: Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine, as Reuben and Simeon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thy family which thou hast begotten after them shall be thine: they shall be called after the name of their brethren in their inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as for me, when I came from Padan, Rachel died by me in the land of Canaan on the way, when there was yet a certain distance to come to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel beheld Joseph's sons, and said, Who are these?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to his father, They are my sons, whomGod has given me here. And he said, Bring them, I pray thee, to me, that I may bless them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the eyes of Israel were heavy from age: he could not see. And he brought them nearer to him; and he kissed them, and embraced them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel said to Joseph, I had not thought to see thy face; and behold,God has let me see also thy seed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph brought them out from his knees, and bowed down with his face to the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph took them both, Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel's left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel's right hand, and brought [them] near to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Israel stretched out his right hand, and laid [it] on Ephraim's head — now he was the younger — and his left hand on Manasseh's head; guiding his hands intelligently, for Manasseh was the firstborn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he blessed Joseph, and said, TheGod before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, theGod that shepherded me all my life long to this day,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the Angel that redeemed me from all evil, bless the lads; and let my name be named upon them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac; and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the land!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Joseph saw that his father laid his right hand on the head of Ephraim, it was evil in his eyes; and he took hold of his father's hand to remove it from Ephraim's head to Manasseh's head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to his father, Not so, my father, for this is the firstborn: put thy right hand on his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his father refused and said, I know, my son, I know: he also will become a people, and he also will be great; but truly his younger brother will be greater than he; and his seed will become the fulness of nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he blessed them that day, saying, In thee will Israel bless, saying,God make thee as Ephraim and Manasseh! And he set Ephraim before Manasseh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel said to Joseph, Behold, I die; andGod will be with you, and bring you again to the land of your fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have given to thee one tract [of land] above thy brethren, which I took out of the hand of the Amorite with my sword and with my bow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob called his sons, and said, Gather yourselves together, and I will tell you what will befall you at the end of days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Assemble yourselves, and hear, ye sons of Jacob, And listen to Israel your father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Reuben, thou art my firstborn, My might, and the firstfruits of my vigour: Excellency of dignity, and excellency of strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Impetuous as the waters, thou shalt have no pre-eminence; Because thou wentest up to thy father's couch: Then defiledst thou [it]: he went up to my bed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simeon and Levi are brethren: Instruments of violence their swords.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul, come not into their council; Mine honour, be not united with their assembly; For in their anger they slew men, And in their wantonness houghed oxen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cursed be their anger, for it [was] violent; And their rage, for it [was] cruel! I will divide them in Jacob, And scatter them in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judah — [as to] thee, thy brethren will praise thee; Thy hand will be upon the neck of thine enemies; Thy father's children will bow down to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judah is a young lion; From the prey, my son, thou art gone up. He stoopeth, he layeth himself down as a lion, And as a lioness: who will rouse him up?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sceptre will not depart from Judah, Nor the lawgiver from between his feet, Until Shiloh come, And to him will be the obedience of peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He bindeth his foal to the vine, And his ass's colt to the choice vine; He washeth his dress in wine, And his garment in the blood of grapes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eyes are red with wine, And the teeth [are] white with milk.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Zebulun will dwell at the shore of the seas; Yea, he will be at the shore of the ships, And his side [toucheth] upon Sidon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Issachar is a bony ass, Crouching down between two hurdles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he saw the rest that it was good, And the land that it was pleasant; And he bowed his shoulder to bear, And was a tributary servant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dan will judge his people, As another of the tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dan will be a serpent on the way, A horned snake on the path, Which biteth the horse's heels, So that the rider falleth backwards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I wait for thy salvation, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gad — troops will rush upon him; But he will rush upon the heel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of Asher, his bread shall be fat, And he will give royal dainties.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Naphtali is a hind let loose; He giveth goodly words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Joseph is a fruitful bough; A fruitful bough by a well; [His] branches shoot over the wall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The archers have provoked him, And shot at, and hated him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his bow abideth firm, And the arms of his hands are supple By the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob. From thence is the shepherd, the stone of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From theGod of thy father, and he will help thee; And from the Almighty, and he will bless thee — With blessings of heaven from above, With blessings of the deep that lieth under, With blessings of the breast and of the womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The blessings of thy father surpass the blessings of my ancestors, Unto the bounds of the everlasting hills: They shall be on the head of Joseph, And on the crown of the head of him that was separated from his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Benjamin — [as] a wolf will he tear to pieces; In the morning he will devour the prey, And in the evening he will divide the booty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father spoke to them; and he blessed them: every one according to his blessing he blessed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he charged them, and said to them, I am gathered to my people: bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is opposite to Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought of Ephron the Hittite along with the field for a possession of a sepulchre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife; there they buried Isaac and Rebecca his wife; and there I buried Leah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The purchase of the field, and of the cave that is in it, was from the children of Heth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jacob had made an end of commanding his sons, he gathered his feet into the bed, and expired, and was gathered to his peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph fell upon his father's face, and wept upon him, and kissed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph commanded his servants the physicians to embalm his father. And the physicians embalmed Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And forty days were fulfilled for him; for so are fulfilled the days of those who are embalmed. And the Egyptians mourned for him seventy days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the days of his mourning were past, Joseph spoke to the house of Pharaoh, saying, If now I have found favour in your eyes, speak, I pray you, in the ears of Pharaoh, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My father made me swear, saying, Behold, I die; in my grave which I have dug myself in the land of Canaan, there shalt thou bury me. And now, let me go up, I pray thee, that I may bury my father; and I will come again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said, Go up and bury thy father, according as he made thee swear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph went up to bury his father; and with him went up all the bondmen of Pharaoh, the elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the house of Joseph, and his brethren, and his father's house; only their little ones, and their flocks, and their herds, they left in the land of Goshen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there went up with him both chariots and horsemen; and the camp was very great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came to the threshing-floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan; and there they lamented with a great and very grievous lamentation; and he made a mourning for his father of seven days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning at the threshing-floor of Atad, and they said, This is a grievous mourning of the Egyptians. Therefore the name of it was called Abel-Mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his sons did to him according as he had commanded them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his sons carried him into the land of Canaan, and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah which Abraham had bought along with the field, for a possession of a sepulchre, of Ephron the Hittite, opposite to Mamre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, after he had buried his father, Joseph returned to Egypt, he and his brethren, and all that had gone up with him to bury his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Joseph's brethren saw that their father was dead, they said, If now Joseph should be hostile to us, and should indeed requite us all the evil that we did to him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sent a messenger to Joseph, saying, Thy father commanded before he died, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus shall ye speak to Joseph: Oh forgive, I pray thee, the transgression of thy brethren, and their sin! for they did evil to thee. And now, we pray thee, forgive the transgression of the servants of theGod of thy father. And Joseph wept when they spoke to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren also went and fell down before his face, and said, Behold, we are thy bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to them, Fear not: am I then in the place ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye indeed meant evil against me:God meant it for good, in order that he might do as [it is] this day, to save a great people alive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, fear not: I will maintain you and your little ones. And he comforted them, and spoke consolingly to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph dwelt in Egypt, he and his father's house; and Joseph lived a hundred and ten years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph saw Ephraim's children of the third [generation]; the sons also of Machir the son of Manasseh were born on Joseph's knees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph said to his brethren, I die; andGod will certainly visit you, and bring you up out of this land, into the land that he swore unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph took an oath of the children of Israel, saying,God will certainly visit you; and ye shall carry up my bones hence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph died, a hundred and ten years old; and they embalmed him; and he was put in a coffin in Egypt.
